label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out how many penguins are more than 5 years old and weigh more than 12 kg. The table has four penguins: Louis, Bernard, Vincent, and Gwen. Let me go through each one by one. First, the criteria: age >5 and weight >12 kg. So both conditions have to be true for a penguin to count. Starting with Louis. His age is 7. That's more than 5, right? And his weight is 11 kg. Hmm, but 11 is less than 12. So Louis doesn't meet the weight requirement. So he doesn't count. Next, Bernard. Age is 5. Wait, the condition is more than 5. So 5 is not more than 5, it's equal. So Bernard's age is exactly 5, so he doesn't meet the age criterion. His weight is 13 kg, which is more than 12. But since his age isn't over 5, he's out. Then Vincent. Age is 9, which is definitely more than 5. His weight is 11 kg. Again, 11 is less than 12. So Vincent's weight doesn't meet the requirement. So he's not counted either. Lastly, Gwen. Age is 8, which is more than 5. Her weight is 15 kg, which is more than 12. Both conditions are satisfied here. So Gwen counts. So looking at all four penguins, only Gwen meets both criteria. Therefore the answer should be 1, which is option A. Wait, but let me double-check to make sure I didn't miss anything. Louis: 7 years, 11 kg. Age okay, weight not. Bernard: 5 years, 13
